question: What are the immediate consequences of unpaid payroll deductions?
answer: The immediate consequences of unpaid payroll deductions are that the CRA will begin to contact your bank, saying to your bank, "please lock the account or please give us money." (Video: FAQ 1 - Consequences of Unpaid Payroll Deductions, Time: 00:00 → 01:39)

question: What should I do if I am behind on payroll source deduction payments?
answer: According to "FAQ 2 - Behind on Payroll Source Deduction Payments" at 00:00 → 01:41, if you are behind on payroll source deduction payments, one possible solution is to sell the corporation to someone who can take over the debt. (Video: FAQ 2 - Behind on Payroll Source Deduction Payments, Time: 00:00 → 01:41, https://rumble.com/v6zl04g-faq-2-what-do-i-do-if-i-am-behind-on-payroll-source-deduction-payments.html)

question: Can I negotiate a payment plan with the CRA?
answer: You can try to negotiate a payment plan with the CRA, but only if you do things in a specific way, such as being completely judgment proof and having your assets hidden in corporations. (Video: FAQ 3 - Negotiate Payment Plan with CRA, Time: 00:00 → 01:43)

question: Do CRA interest and fees keep accruing while negotiating?
answer: Yes, CRA interest and fees keep accruing during negotiation (Video: FAQ 4 - CRA Interest and Fees While Negotiating, Time: 00:00 → 01:40).

question: What are my legal rights when dealing with the CRA?
answer: According to "FAQ 5 - Legal Rights When Dealing with CRA" at 00:00 → 01:35, your legal rights when dealing with the CRA include: the right to remain silent, the right to tell them to leave, the right to put no trespassing notices on your property, the right to call the cops when they're bugging you, the right to file criminal charges, and the right to sue them. (Video: FAQ 5 - Legal Rights When Dealing with CRA, Time: 00:00 → 01:35)

question: How does payroll debt affect my ability to get financing?
answer: According to "FAQ 7 - Payroll Debt and Financing Ability" at 00:00 → 01:35, payroll debt can affect your ability to get financing because the CRA will contact your bank directly with requirement to pay letters, which may impact your financing options. (Video: FAQ 7 - Payroll Debt and Financing Ability, Time: 00:00 → 01:35)

FAQ 8 - How to Walk Away from Payroll Debt

question: How can I walk away from my payroll debt?
answer: According to "FAQ 8 - How to Walk Away from Payroll Debt" at 00:00 → 01:46, you can walk away from your payroll debt by selling your corporation in a share sale, which would eliminate the debt. Additionally, other strategies mentioned include debt assumption by a third party (but only possible with a share sale), legal settlements, or appeals. (Video: FAQ 8 - How to Walk Away from Payroll Debt, Time: 00:00 → 01:46, https://rumble.com/v6zmd6y-faq-8-how-do-i-walk-away-from-my-payroll-debt.html)

question: What happens if I ignore CRA payroll debt?
answer: If you ignore CRA payroll debt, the CRA will contact your bank directly with requirement to pay letters (FAQ 7 - Payroll Debt and Financing Ability, 00:00 → 01:35). Additionally, by the time you are one year behind in payroll, you can expect harassing phone calls and letters to start (FAQ 1 - Consequences of Unpaid Payroll Deductions, 02:35 → 03:33, and FAQ 2 - Behind on Payroll Source Deduction Payments, 00:00 → 01:41). The director of a corporation will also be pursued (FAQ 2 - Behind on Payroll Source Deduction Payments, 01:16 → 03:00).

question: What penalties does the CRA apply for unpaid payroll deductions?
answer: According to "FAQ 1 - Consequences of Unpaid Payroll Deductions" at 00:00 → 01:39, the CRA may contact your bank to lock the account or request money, but does not immediately freeze bank accounts, garnish receivables, or seize assets (https://rumble.com/v6zkyo6-faq-1-what-are-the-consequenses-of-unpaid-payroll-deductions.html). Additionally, at 02:35 → 03:33, it is mentioned that harassing phone calls and letters will start once arrears are identified, typically after being behind on payroll for about a year (https://rumble.com/v6zkyo6-faq-1-what-are-the-consequenses-of-unpaid-payroll-deductions.html).
